author: y4rr
content: Decision: Two approaches by loss complexity. (1) Simple losses (MSE, BCE, L1, Huber, SmoothL1, KLDiv, PoissonNLL, MarginRanking, Hinge, NLL) — refactor backward to use tensor-level ops (sub, mul, div, sigmoid, exp, log, clamp) which already dispatch to GPU. No new kernels needed. (2) Complex losses (CrossEntropy with label smoothing + one_hot indexing, CTC, Cosine/Triplet with multi-input) — these need targeted attention but can still mostly use tensor ops + scatter/gather. Starting with the simple losses first.
